title: Acronia isthmica
intro: Acronia isthmica, also known as Acronia, is a rare and endangered medicinal plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Acronia isthmica include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Acronia isthmica is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, or used as a topical cream or ointment, although its use is limited due to its rarity and potential toxicity.
